revision.comment: [[三珠町]]一部を改変のうえ立項
revision.text:
大塚古墳群（おおつかこふんぐん）は山梨県西八代郡市川三郷町に所在する古墳群。
== 概要 == 旧[[三珠町]]の町域北西部には[[曾根丘陵]]が広がり、甲府市南部（旧[[中道町]]）にある4世紀代の[[甲斐銚子塚古墳]]を中心に数多くの古墳が分布している。当古墳群にも5世紀以降に曽根丘陵から甲府盆地各地へ拡散したとみられる古墳があり、[[5世紀]]の[[帆立貝型古墳]]である大塚古墳や、同じく5世紀末で赤烏元年の[[紀年銘鏡]]が出土して注目されている[[鳥居原狐塚古墳]]などがある。
{{DEFAULTSORT:おおつかこふんくん}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:市川三郷町]] [[Category:山梨県の古墳]]

== 概要 == 旧[[三珠町]]の町域北西部には[[曾根丘陵]]が広がり、甲府市南部（旧[[中道町]]）にある4世紀代の[[甲斐銚子塚古墳]]を中心に数多くの古墳が分布している。当古墳群にも5世紀以降に曽根丘陵から甲府盆地各地へ拡散したとみられる古墳があり、[[5世紀]]の[[帆立貝型古墳]]である大塚古墳や、同じく5世紀末で赤烏元年の[[紀年銘鏡]]が出土して注目されている[[鳥居原狐塚古墳]]などがある。

revision.comment: /* 概要 */ 新節を設置、加筆
revision.text:
'''大塚古墳群'''（おおつかこふんぐん）は[[山梨県]][[西八代郡]][[市川三郷町]]に所在する古墳群。
== 概要 == 旧[[三珠町]]の町域北西部には[[曾根丘陵]]が広がり、甲府市南部（旧[[中道町]]）にある4世紀代の[[甲斐銚子塚古墳]]を中心に数多くの古墳が分布している。当古墳群にも5世紀以降に曽根丘陵から甲府盆地各地へ拡散したとみられる前方後円墳、円墳が分布している。
== 各古墳について == 前方後円墳としてはエモン塚古墳・大塚古墳、[[円墳]]としては伊勢塚古墳・鳥居原狐塚古墳が確認されている。
;大塚古墳 前方後円墳。古墳時代中期の5世紀に属する。
;エモン塚古墳 前方後円墳。
;伊勢塚古墳 円墳。
;鳥居原狐塚古墳 {{main|鳥居原狐塚古墳}} 5世紀末の円墳。赤烏元年の[[紀年銘鏡]]が出土して注目されている。
== 関連項目 == *[[曽根丘陵]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.town.ichikawamisato.yamanashi.jp/50sightsee/50guide/ohtsukakohun.html 観光情報「大塚古墳群」]（市川三郷町）
{{DEFAULTSORT:おおつかこふんくん}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:市川三郷町]] [[Category:山梨県の古墳]]

== 各古墳について == 前方後円墳としてはエモン塚古墳・大塚古墳、円墳としては伊勢塚古墳・鳥居原狐塚古墳が確認されている。

;伊勢塚古墳 円墳。
;鳥居原狐塚古墳 {{main|鳥居原狐塚古墳}} 5世紀末の円墳。赤烏元年の[[紀年銘鏡]]が出土して注目されている。

== 外部リンク == *[http://www.town.ichikawamisato.yamanashi.jp/50sightsee/50guide/ohtsukakohun.html 観光情報「大塚古墳群」]（市川三郷町）

== 各古墳について == 前方後円墳としてはエモン塚古墳・大塚古墳、円墳としては伊勢塚古墳・鳥居原狐塚古墳が確認されている。
;大塚古墳 前方後円墳。[[古墳時代]]中期の5世紀に属する[[帆立貝形古墳]]である。

'''大塚古墳群'''（おおつかこふんぐん）は[[山梨県]][[西八代郡]][[市川三郷町]]に所在する古墳群。
== 概要 == 旧[[三珠町]]の町域北西部には[[曾根丘陵]]が広がり、[[甲府市]]南部（旧[[中道町]]）にある[[4世紀]]代の[[甲斐銚子塚古墳]]を中心に数多くの古墳が分布している。当古墳群にも[[5世紀]]以降に曽根丘陵から[[甲府盆地]]各地へ拡散したとみられる[[前方後円墳]]、[[円墳]]が分布している。
== 各古墳について == 前方後円墳としてはエモン塚古墳・大塚古墳、円墳としては伊勢塚古墳・鳥居原狐塚古墳が確認されている。
;大塚古墳 :前方後円墳。[[古墳時代]]中期の5世紀に属する[[帆立貝形古墳]]である。
;エモン塚古墳 :前方後円墳。
;伊勢塚古墳 :円墳。
;鳥居原狐塚古墳 {{main|鳥居原狐塚古墳}} :5世紀末ころに造営されたとみられる円墳。「赤烏元年五月二十五日」の[[紀年銘鏡]]が出土して注目されている。「[[赤烏]]」は[[呉 (三国)|呉]]の[[孫権]]の治世に行われた[[元号]]で、赤烏元年は西暦238年に相当し、古墳鏡の中でも最古の紀年に属する。

revision.text:
{{Otheruses|the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster located in Ichikawamisato, Yamanashi Prefecture|other similarly named kofun clusters|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster}} '''Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster''' (大塚古墳群) refers to a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) located in {{ill|Ichikawamisato|ja|市川三郷町}}, {{ill|Nishiyatsushiro District|ja|西八代郡}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}.
== Overview == The northern part of Ichikawamisato is characterized by the {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}}, which feature numerous kofun distributed across the region. These include the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}}, a keyhole-shaped tumulus from the 4th century located in southern {{ill|Kōfu|ja|甲府市}}. The Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster contains several keyhole-shaped and round tumuli believed to have spread from the Sone Hills to various parts of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}} after the 5th century.
== Details of the Kofun == The cluster includes the following notable kofun:
;Ōtsuka Kofun :A keyhole-shaped kofun ({{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}) dating back to the mid-Kofun period in the 5th century. ;Emon-zuka Kofun :A keyhole-shaped kofun. ;Isezuka Kofun :A circular kofun. ;Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun {{main|Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun}} :A circular kofun believed to have been constructed around the late 5th century. It is notable for the excavation of a {{ill|dated mirror|ja|紀年銘鏡}} bearing the inscription "May 25, 1st year of Chiwu" (赤烏元年五月二十五日). "Chiwu" (赤烏) was a {{ill|era name|ja|元号}} used during the reign of {{ill|Sun Quan|ja|孫権}} of the {{ill|Wu (Three Kingdoms)|ja|呉 (三国)}}, corresponding to 238 CE. This is one of the earliest dated mirrors associated with kofun.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}}
== External Links == * [http://www.town.ichikawamisato.yamanashi.jp/50sightsee/50guide/ohtsukakohun.html "Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster"] - Ichikawamisato Town Official Site (in Japanese)
{{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:History of Ichikawamisato]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[ja:大塚古墳群]]

{{Otheruses|the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster located in Ichikawamisato, Yamanashi Prefecture|other similarly named kofun clusters|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster}} '''Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster''' (大塚古墳群) refers to a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) located in {{ill|Ichikawamisato|ja|市川三郷町}}, {{ill|Nishiyatsushiro District|ja|西八代郡}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}.
== Overview == The northern part of Ichikawamisato is characterized by the {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}}, which feature numerous kofun distributed across the region. These include the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}}, a keyhole-shaped tumulus from the 4th century located in southern {{ill|Kōfu|ja|甲府市}}. The Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster contains several keyhole-shaped and round tumuli believed to have spread from the Sone Hills to various parts of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}} after the 5th century.
== Details of the Kofun == The cluster includes the following notable kofun:
;Ōtsuka Kofun :A keyhole-shaped kofun ({{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}) dating back to the mid-Kofun period in the 5th century. ;Emon-zuka Kofun :A keyhole-shaped kofun. ;Isezuka Kofun :A circular kofun. ;Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun {{main|Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun}} :A circular kofun believed to have been constructed around the late 5th century. It is notable for the excavation of a {{ill|dated mirror|ja|紀年銘鏡}} bearing the inscription "May 25, 1st year of Chiwu" (赤烏元年五月二十五日). "Chiwu" (赤烏) was a {{ill|era name|ja|元号}} used during the reign of {{ill|Sun Quan|ja|孫権}} of the {{ill|Wu (Three Kingdoms)|ja|呉 (三国)}}, corresponding to 238 CE. This is one of the earliest dated mirrors associated with kofun.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}}
== External Links == * [http://www.town.ichikawamisato.yamanashi.jp/50sightsee/50guide/ohtsukakohun.html "Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster"] - Ichikawamisato Town Official Site (in Japanese)

{{Otheruses|the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster located in Ichikawamisato, Yamanashi Prefecture|other similarly named kofun clusters|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster}} '''Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster''' (大塚古墳群) refers to a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) located in {{ill|Ichikawamisato|ja|市川三郷町}}, {{ill|Nishiyatsushiro District|ja|西八代郡}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}.
== Overview == The northern part of Ichikawamisato is characterized by the {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}}, which feature numerous kofun distributed across the region. These include the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}}, a keyhole-shaped tumulus from the 4th century located in southern {{ill|Kōfu|ja|甲府市}}. The Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster contains several keyhole-shaped and round tumuli believed to have spread from the Sone Hills to various parts of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}} after the 5th century.
== Details of the Kofun == The cluster includes the following notable kofun:
;Ōtsuka Kofun :A keyhole-shaped kofun ({{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}) dating back to the mid-Kofun period in the 5th century. ;Emon-zuka Kofun :A keyhole-shaped kofun. ;Isezuka Kofun :A circular kofun. ;Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun {{main|{{ill|Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun|ja|鳥居原狐塚古墳}}}} :A circular kofun believed to have been constructed around the late 5th century. It is notable for the excavation of a {{ill|dated mirror|ja|紀年銘鏡}} bearing the inscription "May 25, 1st year of Chiwu" (赤烏元年五月二十五日). "Chiwu" (赤烏) was a {{ill|era name|ja|元号}} used during the reign of {{ill|Sun Quan|ja|孫権}} of the {{ill|Wu (Three Kingdoms)|ja|呉 (三国)}}, corresponding to 238 CE. This is one of the earliest dated mirrors associated with kofun.

;Ōtsuka Kofun :A keyhole-shaped kofun ({{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}) dating back to the mid-Kofun period in the 5th century. ;Emon-zuka Kofun :A keyhole-shaped kofun. ;Isezuka Kofun :A circular kofun. ;Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun {{main|{{ill|Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun|ja|鳥居原狐塚古墳}}}} :A circular kofun believed to have been constructed around the late 5th century. It is notable for the excavation of a {{ill|dated mirror|ja|紀年銘鏡}} bearing the inscription "May 25, 1st year of Chiwu" (赤烏元年五月二十五日). "Chiwu" (赤烏) was a {{ill|era name|ja|元号}} used during the reign of {{ill|Sun Quan|ja|孫権}} of the {{ill|Wu (Three Kingdoms)|ja|呉 (三国)}}, corresponding to 238 CE. This is one of the earliest dated mirrors associated with kofun.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}}
== External Links == * [http://www.town.ichikawamisato.yamanashi.jp/50sightsee/50guide/ohtsukakohun.html "Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster"] - Ichikawamisato Town Official Site (in Japanese)

{{Otheruses|the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster located in Ichikawamisato, Yamanashi Prefecture|other similarly named kofun clusters|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster}} '''Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster''' (大塚古墳群) refers to a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) located in {{ill|Ichikawamisato|ja|市川三郷町}}, {{ill|Nishiyatsushiro District|ja|西八代郡}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}.
== Overview == The northern part of Ichikawamisato is characterized by the {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}}, which feature numerous kofun distributed across the region. These include the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}}, a keyhole-shaped tumulus from the 4th century located in southern {{ill|Kōfu|ja|甲府市}}. The Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster contains several keyhole-shaped and round tumuli believed to have spread from the Sone Hills to various parts of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}} after the 5th century.
== Details of the Kofun == The cluster includes the following notable kofun:
;Ōtsuka Kofun :A keyhole-shaped kofun ({{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}) dating back to the mid-Kofun period in the 5th century. ;Emon-zuka Kofun :A keyhole-shaped kofun. ;Isezuka Kofun :A circular kofun. ;Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun {{main|{{ill|Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun|ja|鳥居原狐塚古墳}}}} :A circular kofun believed to have been constructed around the late 5th century. It is notable for the excavation of a {{ill|dated mirror|ja|紀年銘鏡}} bearing the inscription "May 25, 1st year of Chiwu" (赤烏元年五月二十五日). "Chiwu" (赤烏) was a {{ill|era name|ja|元号}} used during the reign of {{ill|Sun Quan|ja|孫権}} of the {{ill|Wu (Three Kingdoms)|ja|呉 (三国)}}, corresponding to 238 CE. This is one of the earliest dated mirrors associated with kofun.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}}
== External Links == * [http://www.town.ichikawamisato.yamanashi.jp/50sightsee/50guide/ohtsukakohun.html "Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster"] - Ichikawamisato Town Official Site (in Japanese)

{{Otheruses|the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster located in Ichikawamisato, Yamanashi Prefecture|other similarly named kofun clusters|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster}} '''Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster''' (大塚古墳群) refers to a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) located in {{ill|Ichikawamisato|ja|市川三郷町}}, {{ill|Nishiyatsushiro District|ja|西八代郡}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}.
== Overview == The northern part of Ichikawamisato is characterized by the {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}}, which feature numerous kofun distributed across the region. These include the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}}, a keyhole-shaped tumulus from the 4th century located in southern {{ill|Kōfu|ja|甲府市}}. The Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster contains several keyhole-shaped and round tumuli believed to have spread from the Sone Hills to various parts of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}} after the 5th century.
== Details of the Kofun == The cluster includes the following notable kofun:
;Ōtsuka Kofun :A keyhole-shaped kofun ({{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}) dating back to the mid-Kofun period in the 5th century. ;Emon-zuka Kofun :A keyhole-shaped kofun. ;Isezuka Kofun :A circular kofun. ;Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun {{main|{{ill|Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun|ja|鳥居原狐塚古墳}}}} :A circular kofun believed to have been constructed around the late 5th century. It is notable for the excavation of a {{ill|dated mirror|ja|紀年銘鏡}} bearing the inscription "May 25, 1st year of Chiwu" (赤烏元年五月二十五日). "Chiwu" (赤烏) was a {{ill|era name|ja|元号}} used during the reign of {{ill|Sun Quan|ja|孫権}} of the {{ill|Wu (Three Kingdoms)|ja|呉 (三国)}}, corresponding to 238 CE. This is one of the earliest dated mirrors associated with kofun.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}}
== External Links == * [http://www.town.ichikawamisato.yamanashi.jp/50sightsee/50guide/ohtsukakohun.html "Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster"] - Ichikawamisato Town Official Site (in Japanese)

The Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster contains several keyhole-shaped and round tumuli believed to have spread from the Sone Hills to various parts of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}} after the 5th century.
== Details of the Kofun == The cluster includes the following notable kofun:
;Ōtsuka Kofun :A keyhole-shaped kofun ({{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}) dating back to the mid-Kofun period in the 5th century. ;Emon-zuka Kofun :A keyhole-shaped kofun. ;Isezuka Kofun :A circular kofun. ;Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun {{main|{{ill|Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun|ja|鳥居原狐塚古墳}}}} :A circular kofun believed to have been constructed around the late 5th century. It is notable for the excavation of a {{ill|dated mirror|ja|紀年銘鏡}} bearing the inscription "May 25, 1st year of Chiwu" (赤烏元年五月二十五日). "Chiwu" (赤烏) was a {{ill|era name|ja|元号}} used during the reign of {{ill|Sun Quan|ja|孫権}} of the {{ill|Wu (Three Kingdoms)|ja|呉 (三国)}}, corresponding to 238 CE. This is one of the earliest dated mirrors associated with kofun.

== Details of the Kofun == The cluster includes the following notable kofun:
;Ōtsuka Kofun :A keyhole-shaped kofun ({{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}) dating back to the mid-Kofun period in the 5th century. ;Emon-zuka Kofun :A keyhole-shaped kofun. ;Isezuka Kofun :A circular kofun. ;Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun {{main|{{ill|Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun|ja|鳥居原狐塚古墳}}}} :A circular kofun believed to have been constructed around the late 5th century. It is notable for the excavation of a {{ill|dated mirror|ja|紀年銘鏡}} bearing the inscription "May 25, 1st year of Chiwu" (赤烏元年五月二十五日). "Chiwu" (赤烏) was a {{ill|era name|ja|元号}} used during the reign of {{ill|Sun Quan|ja|孫権}} of the {{ill|Wu (Three Kingdoms)|ja|呉 (三国)}}, corresponding to 238 CE. This is one of the earliest dated mirrors associated with kofun.

revision.text:
{{Otheruses|the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster located in Ichikawamisato, Yamanashi Prefecture|other similarly named kofun clusters|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster}} '''Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster''' (大塚古墳群) refers to a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) located in {{ill|Ichikawamisato|ja|市川三郷町}}, {{ill|Nishiyatsushiro District|ja|西八代郡}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}.
== Overview == The northern part of Ichikawamisato is characterized by the {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}}, which feature numerous kofun distributed across the region. These include the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}}, a keyhole-shaped tumulus from the 4th century located in southern {{ill|Kōfu|ja|甲府市}}. The Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster contains several keyhole-shaped and round tumuli believed to have spread from the Sone Hills to various parts of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}} after the 5th century.
== Details of the Kofun == The cluster includes the following notable kofun:
;Ōtsuka Kofun :A keyhole-shaped kofun ({{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}) dating back to the mid-Kofun period in the 5th century. ;Emon-zuka Kofun :A keyhole-shaped kofun. ;Isezuka Kofun :A circular kofun. ;Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun {{main|{{ill|Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun|ja|鳥居原狐塚古墳}}}} :A circular kofun believed to have been constructed around the late 5th century. It is notable for the excavation of a {{ill|dated mirror|ja|紀年銘鏡}} bearing the inscription "May 25, 1st year of Chiwu" (赤烏元年五月二十五日). "Chiwu" (赤烏) was a {{ill|era name|ja|元号}} used during the reign of {{ill|Sun Quan|ja|孫権}} of the {{ill|Wu (Three Kingdoms)|ja|呉 (三国)}}, corresponding to 238 CE. This is one of the earliest dated mirrors associated with kofun.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}}
== External Links == * [http://www.town.ichikawamisato.yamanashi.jp/50sightsee/50guide/ohtsukakohun.html "Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster"] - Ichikawamisato Town Official Site (in Japanese)

{{Otheruses|the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster located in Ichikawamisato, Yamanashi Prefecture|other similarly named kofun clusters|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster}} '''Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster''' (大塚古墳群) refers to a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) located in {{ill|Ichikawamisato|ja|市川三郷町}}, {{ill|Nishiyatsushiro District|ja|西八代郡}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}.
== Overview == The northern part of Ichikawamisato is characterized by the {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}}, which feature numerous kofun distributed across the region. These include the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}}, a keyhole-shaped tumulus from the 4th century located in southern {{ill|Kōfu|ja|甲府市}}. The Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster contains several keyhole-shaped and round tumuli believed to have spread from the Sone Hills to various parts of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}} after the 5th century.
== Details of the Kofun == The cluster includes the following notable kofun:
;Ōtsuka Kofun :A keyhole-shaped kofun ({{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}) dating back to the mid-Kofun period in the 5th century. ;Emon-zuka Kofun :A keyhole-shaped kofun. ;Isezuka Kofun :A circular kofun. ;Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun {{main|{{ill|Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun|ja|鳥居原狐塚古墳}}}} :A circular kofun believed to have been constructed around the late 5th century. It is notable for the excavation of a {{ill|dated mirror|ja|紀年銘鏡}} bearing the inscription "May 25, 1st year of Chiwu" (赤烏元年五月二十五日). "Chiwu" (赤烏) was a {{ill|era name|ja|元号}} used during the reign of {{ill|Sun Quan|ja|孫権}} of the {{ill|Wu (Three Kingdoms)|ja|呉 (三国)}}, corresponding to 238 CE. This is one of the earliest dated mirrors associated with kofun.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}}
== External Links == * [http://www.town.ichikawamisato.yamanashi.jp/50sightsee/50guide/ohtsukakohun.html "Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster"] - Ichikawamisato Town Official Site (in Japanese)

;Ōtsuka Kofun :A keyhole-shaped kofun ({{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}) dating back to the mid-Kofun period in the 5th century. ;Emon-zuka Kofun :A keyhole-shaped kofun. ;Isezuka Kofun :A circular kofun. ;Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun {{main|{{ill|Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun|ja|鳥居原狐塚古墳}}}} :A circular kofun believed to have been constructed around the late 5th century. It is notable for the excavation of a {{ill|dated mirror|ja|紀年銘鏡}} bearing the inscription "May 25, 1st year of Chiwu" (赤烏元年五月二十五日). "Chiwu" (赤烏) was a {{ill|era name|ja|元号}} used during the reign of {{ill|Sun Quan|ja|孫権}} of the {{ill|Wu (Three Kingdoms)|ja|呉 (三国)}}, corresponding to 238 CE. This is one of the earliest dated mirrors associated with kofun.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}}

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Completion tier 5]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Otheruses|the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster located in Ichikawamisato, Yamanashi Prefecture|other similarly named kofun clusters|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster}} '''Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster''' (大塚古墳群) refers to a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mounds) located in {{ill|Ichikawamisato|ja|市川三郷町}}, {{ill|Nishiyatsushiro District|ja|西八代郡}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}.
== Overview == The northern part of Ichikawamisato is characterized by the {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}}, which feature numerous kofun distributed across the region. These include the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}}, a keyhole-shaped tumulus from the 4th century located in southern {{ill|Kōfu|ja|甲府市}}. The Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster contains several keyhole-shaped and round tumuli believed to have spread from the Sone Hills to various parts of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}} after the 5th century.
== Details of the Kofun == The cluster includes the following notable kofun:
;Ōtsuka Kofun :A keyhole-shaped kofun ({{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}) dating back to the mid-Kofun period in the 5th century. ;Emon-zuka Kofun :A keyhole-shaped kofun. ;Isezuka Kofun :A circular kofun. ;Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun {{main|{{ill|Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun|ja|鳥居原狐塚古墳}}}} :A circular kofun believed to have been constructed around the late 5th century. It is notable for the excavation of a {{ill|dated mirror|ja|紀年銘鏡}} bearing the inscription "May 25, 1st year of Chiwu" (赤烏元年五月二十五日). "Chiwu" (赤烏) was a {{ill|era name|ja|元号}} used during the reign of {{ill|Sun Quan|ja|孫権}} of the {{ill|Wu (Three Kingdoms)|ja|呉 (三国)}}, corresponding to 238 CE. This is one of the earliest dated mirrors associated with kofun.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}}
== External Links == * [http://www.town.ichikawamisato.yamanashi.jp/50sightsee/50guide/ohtsukakohun.html "Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster"] - Ichikawamisato Town Official Site (in Japanese)

{{Otheruses|the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster located in Ichikawamisato, Yamanashi Prefecture|other similarly named kofun clusters|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster}} '''Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster''' (大塚古墳群) refers to a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mounds) located in {{ill|Ichikawamisato|ja|市川三郷町}}, {{ill|Nishiyatsushiro District|ja|西八代郡}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}.
== Overview == The northern part of Ichikawamisato is characterized by the {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}}, which feature numerous kofun distributed across the region. These include the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}}, a keyhole-shaped tumulus from the 4th century located in southern {{ill|Kōfu|ja|甲府市}}. The Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster contains several keyhole-shaped and round tumuli believed to have spread from the Sone Hills to various parts of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}} after the 5th century.
== Details of the Kofun == The cluster includes the following notable kofun:
;Ōtsuka Kofun :A keyhole-shaped kofun ({{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}) dating back to the mid-Kofun period in the 5th century. ;Emon-zuka Kofun :A keyhole-shaped kofun. ;Isezuka Kofun :A circular kofun. ;Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun {{main|{{ill|Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun|ja|鳥居原狐塚古墳}}}} :A circular kofun believed to have been constructed around the late 5th century. It is notable for the excavation of a {{ill|dated mirror|ja|紀年銘鏡}} bearing the inscription "May 25, 1st year of Chiwu" (赤烏元年五月二十五日). "Chiwu" (赤烏) was a {{ill|era name|ja|元号}} used during the reign of {{ill|Sun Quan|ja|孫権}} of the {{ill|Wu (Three Kingdoms)|ja|呉 (三国)}}, corresponding to 238 CE. This is one of the earliest dated mirrors associated with kofun.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}}
== External Links == * [http://www.town.ichikawamisato.yamanashi.jp/50sightsee/50guide/ohtsukakohun.html "Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster"] - Ichikawamisato Town Official Site (in Japanese)

{{Otheruses|the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster located in Ichikawamisato, Yamanashi Prefecture|other similarly named kofun clusters|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster}} '''Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster''' (大塚古墳群) refers to a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Ichikawamisato|ja|市川三郷町}}, {{ill|Nishiyatsushiro District|ja|西八代郡}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}.
== Overview == The northern part of Ichikawamisato is characterized by the {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}}, which feature numerous kofun distributed across the region. These include the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}}, a keyhole-shaped tumulus from the 4th century located in southern {{ill|Kōfu|ja|甲府市}}. The Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster contains several keyhole-shaped and round tumuli believed to have spread from the Sone Hills to various parts of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}} after the 5th century.
== Details of the Kofun == The cluster includes the following notable kofun:
;Ōtsuka Kofun :A keyhole-shaped kofun ({{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}) dating back to the mid-Kofun period in the 5th century. ;Emon-zuka Kofun :A keyhole-shaped kofun. ;Isezuka Kofun :A circular kofun. ;Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun {{main|{{ill|Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun|ja|鳥居原狐塚古墳}}}} :A circular kofun believed to have been constructed around the late 5th century. It is notable for the excavation of a {{ill|dated mirror|ja|紀年銘鏡}} bearing the inscription "May 25, 1st year of Chiwu" (赤烏元年五月二十五日). "Chiwu" (赤烏) was a {{ill|era name|ja|元号}} used during the reign of {{ill|Sun Quan|ja|孫権}} of the {{ill|Wu (Three Kingdoms)|ja|呉 (三国)}}, corresponding to 238 CE. This is one of the earliest dated mirrors associated with kofun.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}}
== External Links == * [http://www.town.ichikawamisato.yamanashi.jp/50sightsee/50guide/ohtsukakohun.html "Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster"] - Ichikawamisato Town Official Site (in Japanese)

{{Otheruses|the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster located in Ichikawamisato, Yamanashi Prefecture|other similarly named kofun clusters|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster}} '''Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster''' (大塚古墳群) refers to a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Ichikawamisato|ja|市川三郷町}}, {{ill|Nishiyatsushiro District|ja|西八代郡}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}.
== Overview == The northern part of Ichikawamisato is characterized by the {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}}, which feature numerous kofun distributed across the region. These include the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}}, a keyhole-shaped tumulus from the 4th century located in southern {{ill|Kōfu|ja|甲府市}}. The Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster contains several keyhole-shaped and round tumuli believed to have spread from the Sone Hills to various parts of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}} after the 5th century.
== Details of the Kofun == The cluster includes the following notable kofun:
;Ōtsuka Kofun :A keyhole-shaped kofun ({{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}) dating back to the mid-Kofun period in the 5th century. ;Emon-zuka Kofun :A keyhole-shaped kofun. ;Isezuka Kofun :A circular kofun. ;Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun {{main|{{ill|Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun|ja|鳥居原狐塚古墳}}}} :A circular kofun believed to have been constructed around the late 5th century. It is notable for the excavation of a {{ill|dated mirror|ja|紀年銘鏡}} bearing the inscription "May 25, 1st year of Chiwu" (赤烏元年五月二十五日). "Chiwu" (赤烏) was a {{ill|era name|ja|元号}} used during the reign of {{ill|Sun Quan|ja|孫権}} of the {{ill|Wu (Three Kingdoms)|ja|呉 (三国)}}, corresponding to 238 CE. This is one of the earliest dated mirrors associated with kofun.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}}
== External Links == * [http://www.town.ichikawamisato.yamanashi.jp/50sightsee/50guide/ohtsukakohun.html "Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster"] - Ichikawamisato Town Official Site (in Japanese)

{{Otheruses|the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster located in Ichikawamisato, Yamanashi Prefecture|other similarly named kofun clusters|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster}} '''Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster''' (大塚古墳群) refers to a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Ichikawamisato|ja|市川三郷町}}, {{ill|Nishiyatsushiro District|ja|西八代郡}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}.
== Overview == The northern part of Ichikawamisato is characterized by the {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}}, which feature numerous kofun distributed across the region. These include the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}}, a keyhole-shaped tumulus from the 4th century located in southern {{ill|Kōfu|ja|甲府市}}. The Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster contains several keyhole-shaped and round tumuli believed to have spread from the Sone Hills to various parts of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}} after the 5th century.
== Details of the Kofun == The cluster includes the following notable kofun:
;Ōtsuka Kofun :A keyhole-shaped kofun ({{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}) dating back to the mid-Kofun period in the 5th century. ;Emon-zuka Kofun :A keyhole-shaped kofun. ;Isezuka Kofun :A circular kofun. ;Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun {{main|{{ill|Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun|ja|鳥居原狐塚古墳}}}} :A circular kofun believed to have been constructed around the late 5th century. It is notable for the excavation of a {{ill|dated mirror|ja|紀年銘鏡}} bearing the inscription "May 25, 1st year of Chiwu" (赤烏元年五月二十五日). "Chiwu" (赤烏) was a {{ill|era name|ja|元号}} used during the reign of {{ill|Sun Quan|ja|孫権}} of the {{ill|Wu (Three Kingdoms)|ja|呉 (三国)}}, corresponding to 238 CE. This is one of the earliest dated mirrors associated with kofun.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}}
== External Links == * [http://www.town.ichikawamisato.yamanashi.jp/50sightsee/50guide/ohtsukakohun.html "Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster"] - Ichikawamisato Town Official Site (in Japanese)

{{Otheruses|the Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster located in Ichikawamisato, Yamanashi Prefecture|other similarly named kofun clusters|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster}} '''Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster''' (大塚古墳群) refers to a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Ichikawamisato|ja|市川三郷町}}, {{ill|Nishiyatsushiro District|ja|西八代郡}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}.
== Overview == The northern part of Ichikawamisato is characterized by the {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}}, which feature numerous kofun distributed across the region. These include the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}}, a keyhole-shaped tumulus from the 4th century located in southern {{ill|Kōfu|ja|甲府市}}. The Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster contains several keyhole-shaped and round tumuli believed to have spread from the Sone Hills to various parts of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}} after the 5th century.
== Details of the Kofun == The cluster includes the following notable kofun:
;Ōtsuka Kofun :A keyhole-shaped kofun ({{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}) dating back to the mid-Kofun period in the 5th century. ;Emon-zuka Kofun :A keyhole-shaped kofun. ;Isezuka Kofun :A circular kofun. ;Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun {{main|{{ill|Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun|ja|鳥居原狐塚古墳}}}} :A circular kofun believed to have been constructed around the late 5th century. It is notable for the excavation of a {{ill|dated mirror|ja|紀年銘鏡}} bearing the inscription "May 25, 1st year of Chiwu" (赤烏元年五月二十五日). "Chiwu" (赤烏) was a {{ill|era name|ja|元号}} used during the reign of {{ill|Sun Quan|ja|孫権}} of the {{ill|Wu (Three Kingdoms)|ja|呉 (三国)}}, corresponding to 238 CE. This is one of the earliest dated mirrors associated with kofun.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}}

revision.text:
{{Otheruses|山梨県市川三郷町に所在する大塚古墳群|他地域の大塚古墳群|大塚古墳群}} '''大塚古墳群'''（おおつかこふんぐん）は[[山梨県]][[西八代郡]][[市川三郷町]]に所在する古墳群。
== 概要 == 市川三郷町の北部には[[曾根丘陵]]が広がり、[[甲府市]]南部にある[[4世紀]]代の[[甲斐銚子塚古墳]]を中心に数多くの古墳が分布している。当古墳群にも[[5世紀]]以降に曽根丘陵から[[甲府盆地]]各地へ拡散したとみられる[[前方後円墳]]、[[円墳]]が分布している。
== 各古墳について == 前方後円墳としてはエモン塚古墳・大塚古墳、円墳としては伊勢塚古墳・鳥居原狐塚古墳が確認されている。
;大塚古墳 :前方後円墳。[[古墳時代]]中期の5世紀に属する[[帆立貝形古墳]]である。 ;エモン塚古墳 :前方後円墳。 ;伊勢塚古墳 :円墳。 ;鳥居原狐塚古墳 {{main|鳥居原狐塚古墳}} :5世紀末ころに造営されたとみられる円墳。「赤烏元年五月二十五日」の[[紀年銘鏡]]が出土して注目されている。「[[赤烏]]」は[[呉 (三国)|呉]]の[[孫権]]の治世に行われた[[元号]]で、赤烏元年は西暦238年に相当し、古墳鏡の中でも最古の紀年に属する。
== 関連項目 == *[[曽根丘陵]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.town.ichikawamisato.yamanashi.jp/50sightsee/50guide/ohtsukakohun.html 観光情報「大塚古墳群」]（市川三郷町）
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおつかこふんくん}} [[Category:市川三郷町の歴史]] [[Category:山梨県の古墳]] [[Category:古墳群]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11433996]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster''' (大塚古墳群) refers to a group of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) located in [[:en:Ichikawamisato|Ichikawamisato]], [[:en:Nishiyatsushiro District, Yamanashi|Nishiyatsushiro District]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]].
== Overview == The northern part of Ichikawamisato is characterized by the {{ill|Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Hills|qq=}}, which feature numerous kofun distributed across the region. These include the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]], a keyhole-shaped tumulus from the 4th century located in southern [[:en:Kōfu|Kōfu]]. The Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster contains several keyhole-shaped and round tumuli believed to have spread from the Sone Hills to various parts of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōfu Basin|qq=}} after the 5th century.
== Details of the Kofun == The cluster includes the following notable kofun:
;Ōtsuka Kofun :A keyhole-shaped kofun ({{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) dating back to the mid-Kofun period in the 5th century. ;Emon-zuka Kofun :A keyhole-shaped kofun. ;Isezuka Kofun :A circular kofun. ;Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun {{main|{{ill|Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun|ja|鳥居原狐塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun|qq=}}}} :A circular kofun believed to have been constructed around the late 5th century. It is notable for the excavation of a {{ill|dated mirror|ja|紀年銘鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dated mirror|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀年銘資料|1=dated mirror|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605057}} bearing the inscription "May 25, 1st year of Chiwu" (赤烏元年五月二十五日). "Chiwu" (赤烏) was a {{ill|era name|ja|元号|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/era name|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=era name|WD=Q256408}} used during the reign of [[:en:Sun Quan|Sun Quan]] of the [[:en:Eastern Wu|Wu (Three Kingdoms)]], corresponding to 238 CE. This is one of the earliest dated mirrors associated with kofun.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Hills|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.town.ichikawamisato.yamanashi.jp/50sightsee/50guide/ohtsukakohun.html "Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster"] - Ichikawamisato Town Official Site (in Japanese)

== Details of the Kofun == The cluster includes the following notable kofun:

It is notable for the excavation of a {{ill|dated mirror|ja|紀年銘鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dated mirror|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀年銘資料|1=dated mirror|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605057}} bearing the inscription "May 25, 1st year of Chiwu" (赤烏元年五月二十五日). "Chiwu" (赤烏) was a {{ill|era name|ja|元号|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/era name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=era name|WD=Q256408}} used during the reign of [[:en:Sun Quan|Sun Quan]] of the [[:en:Eastern Wu|Wu (Three Kingdoms)]], corresponding to 238 CE. This is one of the earliest dated mirrors associated with kofun.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Hills|qq=}}

;Ōtsuka Kofun :A keyhole-shaped kofun ({{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) dating back to the mid-Kofun period in the 5th century. ;Emon-zuka Kofun :A keyhole-shaped kofun. ;Isezuka Kofun :A circular kofun. ;Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun {{main|{{ill|Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun|ja|鳥居原狐塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun|qq=}}}} :A circular kofun believed to have been constructed around the late 5th century. It is notable for the excavation of a {{ill|dated mirror|ja|紀年銘鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dated mirror|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀年銘資料|1=dated mirror|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605057}} bearing the inscription "May 25, 1st year of Chiwu" (赤烏元年五月二十五日). "Chiwu" (赤烏) was a {{ill|era name|ja|元号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/era name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=era name|WD=Q256408}} used during the reign of [[:en:Sun Quan|Sun Quan]] of the [[:en:Eastern Wu|Wu (Three Kingdoms)]], corresponding to 238 CE. This is one of the earliest dated mirrors associated with kofun.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Hills|qq=}}

;Ōtsuka Kofun :A keyhole-shaped kofun ({{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) dating back to the mid-Kofun period in the 5th century. ;Emon-zuka Kofun :A keyhole-shaped kofun. ;Isezuka Kofun :A circular kofun. ;Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun {{main|{{ill|Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun|ja|鳥居原狐塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun|qq=}}}} :A circular kofun believed to have been constructed around the late 5th century. It is notable for the excavation of a {{ill|dated mirror|ja|紀年銘鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dated mirror|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀年銘資料|1=dated mirror|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605057}} bearing the inscription "May 25, 1st year of Chiwu" (赤烏元年五月二十五日). "Chiwu" (赤烏) was a {{ill|era name|ja|元号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/era name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=era name|WD=Q256408}} used during the reign of [[:en:Sun Quan|Sun Quan]] of the [[:en:Eastern Wu|Wu (Three Kingdoms)]], corresponding to 238 CE. == Overview == The northern part of Ichikawamisato is characterized by the {{ill|Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Hills|qq=}}, which feature numerous kofun distributed across the region. These include the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]], a keyhole-shaped tumulus from the 4th century located in southern [[:en:Kōfu|Kōfu]]. The Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster contains several keyhole-shaped and round tumuli believed to have spread from the Sone Hills to various parts of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōfu Basin|qq=}} after the 5th century.
== Details of the Kofun == The cluster includes the following notable kofun:
;Ōtsuka Kofun :A keyhole-shaped kofun ({{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) dating back to the mid-Kofun period in the 5th century. ;Emon-zuka Kofun :A keyhole-shaped kofun. ;Isezuka Kofun :A circular kofun. ;Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun {{main|{{ill|Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun|ja|鳥居原狐塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun|qq=}}}} :A circular kofun believed to have been constructed around the late 5th century. It is notable for the excavation of a {{ill|dated mirror|ja|紀年銘鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dated mirror|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀年銘資料|1=dated mirror|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605057}} bearing the inscription "May 25, 1st year of Chiwu" (赤烏元年五月二十五日). "Chiwu" (赤烏) was a {{ill|era name|ja|元号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/era name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=era name|WD=Q256408}} used during the reign of [[:en:Sun Quan|Sun Quan]] of the [[:en:Eastern Wu|Wu (Three Kingdoms)]], corresponding to 238 CE. This is one of the earliest dated mirrors associated with kofun.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Hills|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.town.ichikawamisato.yamanashi.jp/50sightsee/50guide/ohtsukakohun.html "Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster"] - Ichikawamisato Town Official Site (in Japanese)
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

{{wikidata link|Q11433997}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:History of Ichikawa-Misato Town]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11433996]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster''' (大塚古墳群) refers to a group of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) located in [[:en:Ichikawamisato|Ichikawamisato]], [[:en:Nishiyatsushiro District, Yamanashi|Nishiyatsushiro District]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]].
== Overview == The northern part of Ichikawamisato is characterized by the {{ill|Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Hills|qq=}}, which feature numerous kofun distributed across the region. These include the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]], a keyhole-shaped tumulus from the 4th century located in southern [[:en:Kōfu|Kōfu]]. The Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster contains several keyhole-shaped and round tumuli believed to have spread from the Sone Hills to various parts of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōfu Basin|qq=}} after the 5th century.
== Details of the Kofun == The cluster includes the following notable kofun:
;Ōtsuka Kofun :A keyhole-shaped kofun ({{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) dating back to the mid-Kofun period in the 5th century. ;Emon-zuka Kofun :A keyhole-shaped kofun. ;Isezuka Kofun :A circular kofun. ;Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun {{main|{{ill|Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun|ja|鳥居原狐塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun|qq=}}}} :A circular kofun believed to have been constructed around the late 5th century. It is notable for the excavation of a {{ill|dated mirror|ja|紀年銘鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dated mirror|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀年銘資料|1=dated mirror|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605057}} bearing the inscription "May 25, 1st year of Chiwu" (赤烏元年五月二十五日). "Chiwu" (赤烏) was a {{ill|era name|ja|元号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/era name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=era name|WD=Q256408}} used during the reign of [[:en:Sun Quan|Sun Quan]] of the [[:en:Eastern Wu|Wu (Three Kingdoms)]], corresponding to 238 CE. This is one of the earliest dated mirrors associated with kofun.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Hills|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.town.ichikawamisato.yamanashi.jp/50sightsee/50guide/ohtsukakohun.html "Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster"] - Ichikawamisato Town Official Site (in Japanese)

;Ōtsuka Kofun :A keyhole-shaped kofun ({{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) dating back to the mid-Kofun period in the 5th century. ;Emon-zuka Kofun :A keyhole-shaped kofun. ;Isezuka Kofun :A circular kofun. ;Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun {{main|{{ill|Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun|ja|鳥居原狐塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun|qq=}}}} :A circular kofun believed to have been constructed around the late 5th century. It is notable for the excavation of a {{ill|dated mirror|ja|紀年銘鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dated mirror|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀年銘資料|1=dated mirror|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605057}} bearing the inscription "May 25, 1st year of Chiwu" (赤烏元年五月二十五日). "Chiwu" (赤烏) was a {{ill|era name|ja|元号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/era name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=era name|WD=Q256408}} used during the reign of {{ill|Sun Quan|en|Sun Quan|WD=Q313327|lt=Sun Quan}} of the {{ill|Eastern Wu|en|Eastern Wu|WD=Q274488|lt=Wu (Three Kingdoms)}}, corresponding to 238 CE. This is one of the earliest dated mirrors associated with kofun.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Hills|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.town.ichikawamisato.yamanashi.jp/50sightsee/50guide/ohtsukakohun.html "Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster"] - Ichikawamisato Town Official Site (in Japanese)
